channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Auschwitz memorial holds observances on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: OSWIECIM, Poland (AP) — The 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz by Soviet troops is being marked on Monday at the site of the former death camp, a ceremony that is widely being treated as the last major observance that any notable number of survivors will be able to attend.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Bulgarian shipping company denies its vessel sabotaged a Baltic Sea cable
item.description: SOFIA, Bulgaria (AP) — A Bulgarian shipping company on Monday denied that one of its ships had intentionally damaged an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island of Gotland.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Belarus strongman extends his rule after an election rejected by opposition and the EU
item.description: Belarus’ authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko has extended his more than three decades in power in Sunday’s orchestrated election that the opposition and the European Union rejected as a sham.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fear and uncertainty after Rwanda-backed rebels claim takeover of eastern Congo's largest city
item.description: Residents in eastern Congo’s largest city of Goma are fleeing after Rwanda-backed rebels claimed to have captured the regional hub from Congolese forces.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kabumba, Chinedu Asadu, Sam Mednick And Christina Malkia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to expect after South Korean president's indictment on rebellion charges
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has become the country's first leader to be indicted, less than two weeks after he was the first to be detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decadeslong conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says US-Colombia agreement in place after back-and-forth over tariffs, immigration
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rubio orders suspension of visa issuance at the US Embassy Bogota
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio ordered the suspension of visa issuance at the U.S. Embassy in Bogota, Colombia, the State Department said Sunday. In a media note, the State Department’s Office of the Spokesperson said the secretary “immediately ordered a suspension of visa issuance at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israel, Hamas reach deal to release hostages and allow Palestinians to return to Gaza Strip
item.description: An agreement has been reached between Israel and Hamas that involves the release of additional hostages and the return of Palestinians to the northern part of the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 22 reported killed in Lebanon before agreement to extend deadline for Israeli forces to withdraw
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Sunday opened fire on protesters demanding their withdrawal in line with a ceasefire agreement, killing at least 22 and injuring 124, Lebanese health officials reported.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why the idea is rejected
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip was met with a hard “no" Sunday from the two U.S. allies along with the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military sets up roadblocks in southern Lebanon and announces it won’t withdraw by deadline
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israel's military Saturday set up roadblocks across border towns and roads in a strategic valley in southern Lebanon, a day before the deadline for it to withdraw from the area under an agreement that halted its war with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South African president signs controversial land seizure bill, eroding private property rights
item.description: Opponents of a controversial South African land expropriation bill are calling the legislation a threat to private ownership after it was endorsed by the country's president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia president decrees emergency powers to restore order in coca region wracked by rebel combat
item.description: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has issued a decree giving him emergency powers to restore order in Catatumbo, a coca-growing region wracked by a deadly turf war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases names of 4 female hostages to be released next, possibly violates deal
item.description: Hamas says it will release four female IDF soldiers including Karina Ariev, Daniella Gilboa, Naama Levy and Liri Albag in second hostage release in a possible violation of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 2,000-year-old statue is found abandoned in a garbage bag in Greece
item.description: A marble statue believed to be more than 2,000 years old has been found in a garbage bag near the city of Thessaloniki in Greece; experts say the piece dates back to the Hellenistic era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin reportedly concerned over Russia's economy ahead of possible Trump tariffs
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is reportedly worried about the state of the country's economy amid threat of tariffs from the U.S. as President Donald Trump returns to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 people are killed in a knife attack in Germany; Scholz says there must be consequences
item.description: A stabbing attack in Germany has left two people dead, including a 2-year-old boy, and three others injured; the suspect was arrested shortly after the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation
